from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
import math
import numpy as np
from wordcloud import WordCloud
from PIL import Image
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pyspark.sql.functions as F
from pyspark.sql.functions import udf, desc, col, isnull, to_timestamp, to_date, trim, year, month,\
     when,months_between, floor, count, lit, max, lower, sum, collect_set, collect_list, explode, datediff
from pyspark.sql.types import StringType, IntegerType, DoubleType, ArrayType, MapType, LongType
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.ml.feature import VectorAssembler, StandardScaler
from pyspark.ml.stat import Correlation
from pyspark.ml.feature import Imputer
from pyspark.ml.functions import vector_to_array
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
from pyspark.ml.regression import RandomForestRegressor
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import RegressionEvaluator

account='eunderwood'
In [2]:
# check if the Spark session is active. If it is activate, close it
try:
    if spark:
        spark.stop()
except:
    pass    

spark = (SparkSession.builder.appName("Multidimensional Data Frame")
        .config("spark.port.maxRetries", "100")
        .config("spark.sql.mapKeyDedupPolicy", "LAST_WIN")  # This configuration allow the duplicate keys in the map data type.
        .config("spark.driver.memory", "8g")
        .getOrCreate())

# confiture the log level (defaulty is WWARN)
spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel('ERROR')

Setting default log level to "WARN".
To adjust logging level use sc.setLogLevel(newLevel). For SparkR, use setLogLevel(newLevel).
26/04/03 13:48:51 WARN NativeCodeLoader: Unable to load native-hadoop library for your platform... using builtin-java classes where applicable
In [3]:
# check unique value in each column. 
def checkUniqueValues(df, top_rows=20):
    for c in df.columns:
        df.groupBy(c).count().orderBy(desc('count')).limit(top_rows).show(top_rows,False)    
        
def missingValueCount(df):
  columns=df.columns
  total_count=df.count()
  dict={}
  i=1
  for c in columns:
    missing_value_count=df.filter(col(c).isNull()).count()
    ratio=round(missing_value_count/total_count, 3)
    dict[c]="Total missing value: "+str(missing_value_count)+ ";  % of Missing Value: "+str(ratio)
  return dict

df = spark.read.csv('WorldSustainabilityDataset.csv', header=True, inferSchema=True)

Correlations with absolute value above 0.3¶

In [12]:
target_and_key_features = [
    'Access to electricity (% of population)', 'Adjusted net national income per capita (annual % growth)',
    'Adjusted net savings, excluding particulate emission damage (% of GNI) ', 'Adjusted savings: carbon dioxide damage (% of GNI) ',
    'Adjusted savings: natural resources depletion (% of GNI)', 'Adjusted savings: net forest depletion (% of GNI)',
    'Adjusted savings: particulate emission damage (% of GNI)', 'Automated teller machines (ATMs) (per 100,000 adults)',
    'Broad money (% of GDP)', 'Children out of school (% of primary school age)', 'Compulsory education, duration (years) ',
    'Cost of business start-up procedures, female (% of GNI per capita)', 'Cost of business start-up procedures, male (% of GNI per capita)',
    'Exports of goods and services (% of GDP) ', 'Final consumption expenditure (% of GDP)', 'GDP per capita (current US$) ',
    'General government final consumption expenditure (% of GDP) ', 'Gross national expenditure (% of GDP)',
    'Imports of goods and services (% of GDP) ', 'Inflation, consumer prices (annual %)',
    'Primary completion rate, total (% of relevant age group)', 'Proportion of seats held by women in national parliaments (%)',
    'Pupil-teacher ratio, primary', 'Renewable electricity output (% of total electricity output) ',
    'Renewable energy consumption (% of total final energy consumption) ', 'School enrollment, preprimary (% gross) ',
    'School enrollment, primary (% gross) ', 'School enrollment, secondary (% gross)', 'Trade (% of GDP)',
    'Women Business and the Law Index Score (scale 1-100)', 'Prevalence of undernourishment (%) ',
    'Proportion of population below international poverty line (%) ', 'Proportion of population covered by at least a 2G mobile network (%) ',
    'Proportion of population covered by at least a 3G mobile network (%) ', 'Proportion of population using basic drinking water services (%)',
    'Unemployment rate, male (%)', 'Unemployment rate, women (%)',
    'Annual production-based emissions of carbon dioxide (CO2), measured in million tonnes', 'Gini index (World Bank estimate)',
    'Individuals using the Internet (% of population) ', 'Life expectancy at birth, total (years)', 'Population, total ',
    'Rural population (% of total population) ', 'Total natural resources rents (% of GDP) ', 'Urban population (% of total population) '
]

df_clean = df.select(target_and_key_features).dropna()

assembler = VectorAssembler(inputCols=target_and_key_features, outputCol="features", handleInvalid="skip")
df_vector = assembler.transform(df_clean).select("features")

matrix = Correlation.corr(df_vector, "features").collect()[0][0]
cor_np = matrix.toArray()

# Convert to Pandas for visualization
corr_df = pd.DataFrame(cor_np, columns=target_and_key_features, index=target_and_key_features)

# Filter: Keep only rows/cols that have at least one correlation > 0.3 (excluding self-correlation)
keep = (np.abs(corr_df) > 0.3).sum() > 1
filtered_corr = corr_df.loc[keep, keep]

# Create Masks: Hide weak correlations and redundant upper triangle
mask_weak = np.abs(filtered_corr) < 0.3
mask_tri = np.triu(np.ones_like(filtered_corr, dtype=bool))
combined_mask = mask_weak | mask_tri

# Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(28, 22))
sns.heatmap(filtered_corr, 
            mask=combined_mask, 
            annot=True, 
            fmt=".2f", 
            cmap='coolwarm', 
            vmin=-1, vmax=1, 
            center=0,
            linewidths=.5,
            cbar_kws={"shrink": 0.8})

plt.title("Significant Pairwise Correlations (> 0.3)", fontsize=18)
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right')
plt.show()
In [13]:
# smaller, thematic list of correlations
infrastructure_cols = [
    'Access to electricity (% of population)',
    'Automated teller machines (ATMs) (per 100,000 adults)',
    'Individuals using the Internet (% of population) ',
    'Proportion of population covered by at least a 3G mobile network (%) ',
    'GDP per capita (current US$) '
]

# Slice the existing matrix and plot
infra_corr = corr_df.loc[infrastructure_cols, infrastructure_cols]
sns.heatmap(infra_corr, annot=True, cmap='vlag', center=0)
plt.show()
In [14]:
target = 'Life expectancy at birth, total (years)'

# Sort correlations and drop the target's self-correlation (1.0)
target_corr = corr_df[target].sort_values(ascending=False).drop(target)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
target_corr.plot(kind='barh', color=(target_corr > 0).map({True: 'steelblue', False: 'indianred'}))
plt.title(f"Variables Correlated with {target}")
plt.axvline(x=0, color='black', linestyle='-', linewidth=1)
plt.show()
In [53]:
# Verify the relationship between life expectancy and GDP per capita
sns.jointplot(x='GDP per capita (current US$) ',
              y='Life expectancy at birth, total (years)',
              data=df_clean.toPandas(), 
              kind='reg', 
              color='purple')
plt.xlim(0, 15000)
plt.show()
In [16]:
pairs = corr_df.unstack().reset_index()
pairs.columns = ['Feature 1', 'Feature 2', 'Correlation']

# Filter out self-correlations and duplicates, then show top strongest
top_pairs = pairs[(pairs['Correlation'] < 1) & (pairs['Correlation'].abs() > 0.7)]
print(top_pairs.sort_values(by='Correlation', ascending=False).drop_duplicates(subset=['Correlation']))

Correlations with life expectancy¶

In [17]:
# select target
target_col = 'Life expectancy at birth, total (years)'
life_expect_corr = corr_df[[target_col]].sort_values(by=target_col, ascending=False)

# Filter for significant drivers (absolute value of correlation > 0.4)
significant_drivers = life_expect_corr[np.abs(life_expect_corr[target_col]) > 0.4]

# plot
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 12))
sns.barplot(x=significant_drivers[target_col], y=significant_drivers.index, palette='RdYlGn_r')
plt.title(f"Top Predictors for {target_col}", fontsize=15)
plt.xlabel("Pearson Correlation Coefficient")
plt.grid(axis='x', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)
plt.show()

/tmp/ipykernel_1937149/3839403852.py:10: FutureWarning: 

Passing `palette` without assigning `hue` is deprecated and will be removed in v0.14.0. Assign the `y` variable to `hue` and set `legend=False` for the same effect.

  sns.barplot(x=significant_drivers[target_col], y=significant_drivers.index, palette='RdYlGn_r')

Handling multicollinearity¶

In [19]:
# Get absolute correlation matrix (from existing corr_df)
corr_matrix = corr_df.abs()

# Select the upper triangle of the correlation matrix
upper = corr_matrix.where(np.triu(np.ones(corr_matrix.shape), k=1).astype(bool))

# index of columns with correlation greater than 0.5
to_drop = [column for column in upper.columns if any(upper[column] > 0.5) and column != target_col]

final_feature_list = [col for col in target_and_key_features if col not in to_drop]

print(f"Original feature count: {len(target_and_key_features)}")
print(f"Dropped {len(to_drop)} highly correlated features.")
print(f"Final feature count: {len(final_feature_list)}")

if target_col in final_feature_list:
    remaining_corr = corr_df.loc[final_feature_list, target_col].sort_values(ascending=False)
    print("\nTop remaining features correlated with Life Expectancy:")
    print(remaining_corr.head(10))

Dropped 36 highly correlated features.
Final feature count: 9

In [20]:
top_features = [
    'Life expectancy at birth, total (years)',
    'Access to electricity (% of population)',
    'Broad money (% of GDP)',
    'Adjusted savings: natural resources depletion (% of GNI)',
    'Children out of school (% of primary school age)'
]

eda_df = df_clean.select(top_features).toPandas()
In [21]:
def remove_outliers_iqr(df, columns):
    df_final = df.copy()
    for col in columns:
        Q1 = df_final[col].quantile(0.25)
        Q3 = df_final[col].quantile(0.75)
        IQR = Q3 - Q1
        
        lower_bound = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
        upper_bound = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
        
        df_final = df_final[(df_final[col] >= lower_bound) & (df_final[col] <= upper_bound)]
        
    return df_final
In [22]:
eda_df_clean = remove_outliers_iqr(eda_df, top_features)

# Compare results
print(f"Rows before: {len(eda_df)}")
print(f"Rows after: {len(eda_df_clean)}")
print(f"Removed {len(eda_df) - len(eda_df_clean)} outlier rows.")

Rows before: 36
Rows after: 27
Removed 9 outlier rows.

    sns.regplot(x=col, y='Life expectancy at birth, total (years)', 
                data=eda_df_clean, ax=ax, scatter_kws={'alpha':0.3}, line_kws={'color':'red'})
    ax.set_title(f'Impact of {col} on Life Expectancy')

In [25]:
# life expectancy vs CO2 dmg

target_col = 'Life expectancy at birth, total (years)'
co2_col = 'Adjusted savings: carbon dioxide damage (% of GNI) '

plot_df = df.select(target_col, co2_col).dropna().toPandas()

plot_df_clean = remove_outliers_iqr(plot_df, [target_col, co2_col])

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.regplot(
    x=co2_col, 
    y=target_col, 
    data=plot_df_clean, 
    scatter_kws={'alpha': 0.3, 'color': 'seagreen'}, 
    line_kws={'color': 'darkred', 'lw': 2}
)

plt.title('Relationship: Life Expectancy vs. CO2 Damage (Normalized)', fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel('CO2 Damage (% of GNI)', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Life Expectancy (Years)', fontsize=12)
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)

plt.show()

# Print the numerical correlation for the record
print(f"Correlation coefficient: {plot_df_clean[target_col].corr(plot_df_clean[co2_col]):.3f}")

Correlation coefficient: 0.042

no correlation between CO2 damage & life expectancy

bin_edges = [98, 98.25, 98.5, 98.75, 99, 99.25, 99.5, 100]
bin_labels = [
    '98.0-98.25%', '98.25-98.5%', '98.5-98.75%', 
    '98.75-99.0%', '99.0-99.25%', '99.25-99.5%', '99.5-100%'
]

eda_df_clean['Electricity_Bin_Ultra_Fine'] = pd.cut(
    eda_df_clean['Access to electricity (% of population)'], 
    bins=bin_edges, 
    labels=bin_labels,
    include_lowest=True
)

plt.figure(figsize=(11, 7))
sns.boxplot(
    x='Electricity_Bin_Ultra_Fine', 
    y='Life expectancy at birth, total (years)', 
    data=eda_df_clean.dropna(subset=['Electricity_Bin_Ultra_Fine']), # Focus only on this range
    palette='magma'
)

plt.title('Impact of Universal Electricity Access (%)', fontsize=16)
plt.xlabel('Electricity Access Micro-Bins', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Life Expectancy (Years)', fontsize=12)
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("Sample size and mean Life Expectancy per micro-bin:")
print(eda_df_clean.groupby('Electricity_Bin_Ultra_Fine')['Life expectancy at birth, total (years)'].agg(['count', 'mean']))

In [36]:
features_to_track = [elec_col, money_col, res_col, school_col]
temp_df = df.select(features_to_track + [target_col, 'Year']).dropna().toPandas()

# Calculate correlation for each feature by year, store results in a dictionary
yearly_results = {}

for feat in features_to_track:
    # absolute value 
    yearly_results[feat] = temp_df.groupby('Year').apply(lambda x: x[feat].corr(x[target_col])).abs()

corr_history = pd.DataFrame(yearly_results)

# Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))

# Define distinct colors and markers for clarity
colors = ['gold', 'forestgreen', 'royalblue', 'darkorange']
markers = ['o', 's', '^', 'D']

for i, feat in enumerate(features_to_track):
    plt.plot(corr_history.index, corr_history[feat], 
             marker=markers[i], color=colors[i], 
             linewidth=2, label=feat.split(' (')[0]) # Shorten labels for legibility

# Formatting the X-Axis
start_year = int(corr_history.index.min())
end_year = int(corr_history.index.max())
plt.xticks(range(start_year, end_year + 1, 2))

plt.title('Evolution of Predictor Strength: 2000-2018', fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel('Absolute Pearson Correlation ($|r|$)')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylim(0, 1.0) # Show full range of possible correlation strength
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left') # Move legend outside
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

  yearly_results[feat] = temp_df.groupby('Year').apply(lambda x: x[feat].corr(x[target_col])).abs()

Modeling¶

Random Forest¶

In [30]:
#final features with no multicollinearity
features = [
    'Access to electricity (% of population)',
    'Broad money (% of GDP)',
    'Adjusted savings: natural resources depletion (% of GNI)',
    'Children out of school (% of primary school age)',
    'Proportion of seats held by women in national parliaments (%)',
    'Adjusted net national income per capita (annual % growth)',
    'Adjusted savings: carbon dioxide damage (% of GNI) '
]
target = 'Life expectancy at birth, total (years)'

train_df, test_df = df.select(features + [target]).dropna().randomSplit([0.8, 0.2], seed=42)

assembler = VectorAssembler(inputCols=features, outputCol="raw_features")
scaler = StandardScaler(inputCol="raw_features", outputCol="scaled_features")

rf = RandomForestRegressor(
    featuresCol="scaled_features", 
    labelCol=target, 
    numTrees=100,      # Number of trees 
    maxDepth=15,       # Depth of trees 
    seed=42
)

# Build and Train the Pipeline
pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[assembler, scaler, rf])
model = pipeline.fit(train_df)

# Predictions
predictions = model.transform(test_df)

# Evaluate Performance
evaluator_rmse = RegressionEvaluator(labelCol=target, predictionCol="prediction", metricName="rmse")
evaluator_r2 = RegressionEvaluator(labelCol=target, predictionCol="prediction", metricName="r2")

rmse = evaluator_rmse.evaluate(predictions)
r2 = evaluator_r2.evaluate(predictions)

print(f"Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE): {rmse:.3f} years")
print(f"R-Squared (R2): {r2:.3f}")

WARNING: An illegal reflective access operation has occurred                    
WARNING: Illegal reflective access by org.apache.spark.util.SizeEstimator$ (file:/opt/conda/envs/BigDataEnv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pyspark/jars/spark-core_2.12-3.5.5.jar) to field java.nio.charset.Charset.name
WARNING: Please consider reporting this to the maintainers of org.apache.spark.util.SizeEstimator$
WARNING: Use --illegal-access=warn to enable warnings of further illegal reflective access operations
WARNING: All illegal access operations will be denied in a future release

Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE): 2.039 years
R-Squared (R2): 0.941

GBT¶

In [31]:
from pyspark.ml.regression import GBTRegressor
gbt = GBTRegressor(
    featuresCol="scaled_features", 
    labelCol=target, 
    maxIter=100,        # Number of boosting iterations
    maxDepth=5,        # tried shallower trees
    stepSize=0.1,      # Learning rate
    seed=42
)

gbt_pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[assembler, scaler, gbt])
gbt_model = gbt_pipeline.fit(train_df)

gbt_predictions = gbt_model.transform(test_df)

# evaluate GBT performance
rmse_gbt = evaluator_rmse.evaluate(gbt_predictions)
r2_gbt = evaluator_r2.evaluate(gbt_predictions)

print(f"--- GBT Performance ---")
print(f"RMSE: {rmse_gbt:.3f} years")
print(f"R-Squared: {r2_gbt:.3f}")

--- GBT Performance ---
RMSE: 2.413 years
R-Squared: 0.918

Evaluation¶

In [32]:
# Create a simple comparison table
comparison_data = {
    'Metric': ['RMSE', 'R2'],
    'Random Forest': [rmse, r2],
    'Gradient Boosting': [rmse_gbt, r2_gbt]
}

comparison_df = pd.DataFrame(comparison_data)
print(comparison_df)

  Metric  Random Forest  Gradient Boosting
0   RMSE       2.038644           2.413415
1     R2       0.941336           0.917785
In [54]:
import builtins # to prevent Spark function collisions
p_df = gbt_predictions.select(target, "prediction").toPandas()

plt.figure(figsize=(5, 5))
sns.scatterplot(x=target, y="prediction", data=p_df, alpha=0.4, color='darkgreen')

# Calculate bounds using Python's max/min
actual_max = p_df[target].max()
pred_max = p_df["prediction"].max()
actual_min = p_df[target].min()
pred_min = p_df["prediction"].min()

max_val = builtins.max(actual_max, pred_max)
min_val = builtins.min(actual_min, pred_min)

plt.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], color='red', linestyle='--', label='Perfect Prediction')

plt.title('GBT Model: Actual vs. Predicted Life Expectancy', fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel('Actual Life Expectancy (Years)')
plt.ylabel('Predicted Life Expectancy (Years)')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()
In [43]:
p_df['residual'] = p_df[target] - p_df['prediction']

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

# Residuals vs. Predicted
plt.subplot(1, 2, 1)
sns.scatterplot(x='prediction', y='residual', data=p_df, alpha=0.4, color='darkgreen')
plt.axhline(y=0, color='red', linestyle='--')
plt.title('GBT Residuals vs. Predicted Values')
plt.xlabel('Predicted Life Expectancy (Years)')
plt.ylabel('Residual (Actual - Prediction)')

# Distribution of Errors (Checking for Bias)
plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)
sns.histplot(p_df['residual'], kde=True, color='darkgreen')
plt.axvline(x=0, color='red', linestyle='--')
plt.title('Distribution of Model Errors')
plt.xlabel('Residual (Years)')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Statistical Summary of Errors
mae = p_df['residual'].abs().mean()
print(f"GBT Mean Absolute Error (MAE): {mae:.3f} years")

GBT Mean Absolute Error (MAE): 1.485 years
In [50]:
rf_predictions = model.transform(test_df)

# calculate Residuals
rf_p_df = rf_predictions.select(target, "prediction").toPandas()
rf_p_df['residual'] = rf_p_df[target] - rf_p_df['prediction']

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

# Residuals vs. Predicted
plt.subplot(1, 2, 1)
sns.scatterplot(x='prediction', y='residual', data=rf_p_df, alpha=0.4, color='darkblue')
plt.axhline(y=0, color='red', linestyle='--')
plt.title('RF Residuals vs. Predicted Values')
plt.xlabel('Predicted Life Expectancy (Years)')
plt.ylabel('Residual (Actual - Prediction)')

# Distribution of Errors (Checking for Bias)
plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)
sns.histplot(rf_p_df['residual'], kde=True, color='darkblue')
plt.axvline(x=0, color='red', linestyle='--')
plt.title('Distribution of Model Errors')
plt.xlabel('Residual (Years)')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Statistical Summary of Errors
mae = rf_p_df['residual'].abs().mean()
print(f"Random Forest Mean Absolute Error (MAE): {mae:.3f} years")

Random Forest Mean Absolute Error (MAE): 1.420 years
In [45]:
plt.figure(figsize=(5, 5))
sns.scatterplot(x=target, y="prediction", data=rf_p_df, alpha=0.4, color='darkblue')

# Calculate bounds using Python's max/min
actual_max = rf_p_df[target].max()
pred_max = rf_p_df["prediction"].max()
actual_min = rf_p_df[target].min()
pred_min = rf_p_df["prediction"].min()

max_val = builtins.max(actual_max, pred_max)
min_val = builtins.min(actual_min, pred_min)

plt.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], color='red', linestyle='--', label='Perfect Prediction')

plt.title('RF Model: Actual vs. Predicted Life Expectancy', fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel('Actual Life Expectancy (Years)')
plt.ylabel('Predicted Life Expectancy (Years)')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()
In [ ]:
